title: Mildly Citrusy Kenyan Chicken Pilaf
description: A festive Kenyan dish featuring chicken, rice, and a hint of citrus, made with rice vinegar and cooked to perfection using the bain-marie method, perfect for dairy-free diets. This dish is crisp and full of flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chicken to the saucepan and cook until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the rice to the saucepan and stir to combine with the chicken and onion mixture.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the rice vinegar, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper.
Add the vinegar mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Add the water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly, then fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve.
